release_notes: Animations now traverse the whole canvas in a different random sequence per play. The previous _wander_xy-based spots clung to the figure's emerge side, so even with bigger jitter the figure stayed in roughly the same lane every play. New _random_spots(button, w, h, n, side) helper samples n distinct spots from a fixed candidate set covering all four corners (top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right), the top/bottom centers, and the two mid-edges — eight regions total, chosen in random order per-play with ±30px x / ±25px y jitter on each. The figure now visibly walks across the canvas to hit those spots; on plays where consecutive spots straddle the button, the button mask briefly hides the figure mid-walk, reading as 'duck behind the button' rather than a glitch. Cartwheel uses same_y=True so launch and far still share a horizontal line, and the rotation direction is now derived from the actual launch→far motion instead of the emerge side (otherwise the figure could end up rolling backwards). Stretches, jumping jacks, weights, dance, and cartwheel all updated to use _random_spots.
